tooltips.expectancy: The average amount you can expect to win (or lose) per trade.
tooltips.profitFactor: The total gross profit divided by the total gross loss.
tooltips.maxDrawdown: The maximum observed loss from a peak to a trough of a portfolio.
tooltips.winRate: The percentage of trades that resulted in a profit.
tooltips.trades: The total number of closed trades analyzed.

strategyScore.title: A strategy can make money… and still be wrong.
strategyScore.desc: Most strategies never reach this level. If your sample isn't large enough, you're just guessing.
strategyScore.reliabilityNote: Small samples invalidate any positive conclusion. Stop trading on hope.

faq.label: Technical Rigor
faq.title: Infrastructure FAQs
faq.a1: It's a weighting of Profit Factor, Expectancy, Max Drawdown, and Coefficient of Variation, adjusted by data sample size.
faq.q2: Is it compatible with my broker?
faq.a2: We support 99% of brokers via standard exports from MT4, MT5, and generic CSV files.
faq.a3: Absolutely. Processing is local in your browser. Your trade data is not sent to or stored on our servers unless you save the report.
faq.a4: For security. Requesting broker credentials is an unnecessary risk. The exported file is sufficient for professional analysis.
faq.q5: How many trades do I need?
faq.a5: We recommend +100 trades. With fewer than 30, any result is mathematically indistinguishable from luck.
faq.a6: It means the probability of your winning streak being pure luck is extremely low under Monte Carlo simulations.
faq.a7: Basic analysis is free. Portfolio tracking and advanced simulation features require the Pro plan.

finalCta.label: Final Decision
finalCta.title: Stop guessing before it costs you real money.
finalCta.desc: Verify if your strategy is real… before it fails.
finalCta.note: Every day you don't verify, you're risking capital blindly.
finalCta.pressure: Most traders never verify this. That’s why they fail.

ranking.top5: This strategy performs better than 95% of tested strategies.
ranking.top20: This strategy performs better than 80% of tested strategies.
ranking.top40: This strategy performs better than 60% of tested strategies.
ranking.bottom50: This strategy performs better than 30% of tested strategies.
ranking.bottom20: This strategy is in the bottom 20% of tested strategies.

narrative.elite: Your strategy shows exceptional statistical robustness and risk control.
narrative.strong: Your strategy shows signs of a statistical edge based on historical trades.
narrative.promising: Promising performance metrics. Some optimizations could lead to elite results.
narrative.unstable: Profitable but vulnerable. Review drawdown periods to identify potential risks.
narrative.none: Current data doesn't show a reliable statistical edge.
narrative.gainR: Your strategy earns +{val}R per trade on average — that's a real statistical edge.
narrative.breakEvenR: Your strategy breaks even with 0.00R per trade. The edge needs improvement.
narrative.lossR: Your strategy loses {val}R per trade on average — review entry/exit rules.
narrative.highPf: Profit Factor {val} — your strategy returns {val} units for every 1 unit lost. A solid edge.
narrative.marginalPf: Profit Factor {val} is profitable but marginal. Small improvements in R:R or entry timing could significantly boost returns.
narrative.lowPf: A Profit Factor below 1.0 means losses outweigh gains. Review entry timing and stop loss placement before live trading.

scoreExplanation.title: Strategy Score Components
scoreExplanation.desc: The Strategy Score combines several statistical metrics to evaluate if a trading strategy shows a potential edge based on historical operations.

weights.pfTip: Measures total profit vs total loss. Target > 1.5.
weights.expTip: Average R-unit profit per trade. Essential for edge quality.
weights.ddTip: Max peak-to-valley loss. Lower is safer.
weights.consTip: How stable your equity growth is over time.

strongEdge.label: Strong Statistical Edge Detected
strongEdge.explanation: Your strategy shows exceptional statistical robustness and risk control.

weakEdge.label: Limited Statistical Edge
weakEdge.explanation: Your strategy demonstrates a small edge that may be vulnerable to market changes.

unstableEdge.label: Unstable Performance
unstableEdge.explanation: The performance varies significantly across cycles; the edge is not yet stable.

noEdge.label: No Reliable Edge Detected
noEdge.explanation: Current data does not demonstrate a reliable statistical advantage.

insufficientSample.label: Insufficient Data Sample
insufficientSample.explanation: At least 30 trades are required for a statistically relevant diagnosis.

interpretation.title: What This Means
strongEdge: Risk of ruin is exceptionally low
weakEdge: Sensitive to slippage or high spreads
insufficientSample: Metrics are prone to high variance

nextSteps.title: Suggested Next Step
nextSteps.strongEdge: Run a Prop Firm simulation to see if your strategy can scale professionally.
nextSteps.weakEdge: Conduct a deep dive into losing trades to refine your entry criteria.
nextSteps.unstableEdge: Reduce position sizes until performance stabilizes across the next 30 trades.
nextSteps.noEdge: Review your core logic; the current data suggest a negative edge.
nextSteps.insufficientSample: Continue trading with minimum risk until reaching 50-100 trades.

tooltips.score: The Strategy Score combines metrics like Profit Factor and Expectancy to assess strategy quality.
tooltips.pf: The ratio of gross profit to gross loss. A PF > 1.4 suggests a healthy statistical edge.
tooltips.expectancy: The average amount you expect to win (or lose) per dollar risked.
tooltips.maxDrawdown: The peak-to-valley percentage drop in your account equity during analysis.

edgeConfidence.title: Edge Confidence
edgeConfidence.tooltip: Edge Confidence estimates how statistically reliable your strategy's advantage is.

sqn.title: SQN (System Quality Number)
sqn.tooltip: SQN measures strategy quality based on expectancy and standard deviation normalized by trade count.

zscore.title: Z-Score (Correlation)
zscore.tooltip: Z-Score measures trade dependency. A high score suggests wins/losses are clustered.

monteCarlo.title: Monte Carlo Analysis
monteCarlo.tooltip: Simulates thousands of variations of your trade sequence to identify worst-case outcomes and survival probability.

intelligence.title: Strategy Intelligence
intelligence.subtitle: Advanced visual insights into edge stability and future probability ranges.

payoffDistribution.title: Normalized Payoff Distribution
payoffDistribution.subtitle: Returns in R-units to visualize skewness and tail risk.

equityFan.title: Equity Probability Cone
equityFan.subtitle: Simulation of future outcomes showing confidence bands.

comparison.title: Strategy Comparison
comparison.subtitle: Side-by-side evaluation of up to 3 strategies to identify the most robust edge.

expertTips.title: Expert Recommendations
expertTips.tipZScoreHigh: Results appear clustered. Consider reducing size after losses or reviewing if your strategy overtrades in certain conditions.
expertTips.tipSQNLow: Strategy edge is statistically weak. Increasing sample size or refining entry criteria may improve reliability.
expertTips.tipMCDDHigh: Risk exposure may be higher than historical results suggest. Monte Carlo Worst Case ({mcDD}) is significantly larger than Historical DD ({histDD}).
expertTips.tipSampleSizeLow: Sample size is still small. Avoid over-leveraging until you reach at least 50-100 trades for higher statistical confidence.

edgeQuality.title: Edge Quality
edgeQuality.tipSolid: Run a Monte Carlo analysis to validate if this edge holds under simulated variations.
edgeQuality.tipMarginal: Focus on improving the Risk:Reward. Even a small increase can push PF above 1.5.
edgeQuality.tipNone: Review your last 20 losing trades to identify repeated entry errors.

riskProfile.title: Risk Profile
riskProfile.tipControlled: Verify if this drawdown remains stable across different market sessions.
riskProfile.tipModerate: Add a daily loss limit to prevent drawdown streaks that erode the account.
riskProfile.tipHigh: Reduce your position sizes by 50% until Max DD stabilizes below 20%.

sample.title: Sample Reliability
sample.tipRobust: Monitor if metrics drift as you add the next 50 trades to ensure stability.
sample.tipModerate: Continue trading the same plan without changes until reaching 100 trades.
sample.tipLow: Focus on execution consistency rather than results until the sample exceeds 50.

location.title: Edge Location
location.tipOptimal: Consider trading ONLY during the {session} session on your best days.
location.tipInsufficient: Ensure your CSV includes 'symbol' and 'datetime' columns for deep analysis.

weekday.title: Performance by Weekday
weekday.explanation: Your strategy loses money on {day}s with a negative expectancy.
weekday.positive: Your performance is exceptionally solid on {day}s.
weekday.tip: You may want to review trades taken on {day}s.

sessions.title: Session Performance
sessions.explanation: Your best performance occurs during the {session} session.
sessions.warning: The {session} session is currently your least profitable period.
sessions.tip: Consider focusing your trading during these hours for maximum edge.

drawdown.title: Drawdown Profile
drawdown.explanation: Your drawdowns are {level} than average for similar strategies.
drawdown.tip: Review your position sizing rules to ensure drawdown stays below 20%.

winrate.title: Win Rate dependency
winrate.tip: Ensure your stop losses are always respected to protect this edge.

hero.headline: Strategy Analyzer — Discover Your Real Trading Edge
hero.subheadline: Stop guessing if your strategy works. Upload your trade history and get a professional analysis in under 60 seconds. Calculate your Win Rate, Profit Factor and Expectancy for free.

steps.title: Export CSV Data
steps.desc: Export your trade history from your broker or platform in CSV or Excel format. We support MT4, MT5 and standard formats.

steps.title: Automatic Parsing
steps.desc: Upload your file to NodoQuant. Our system normalizes dates, symbols and P&L into R-units for standardized analysis.

steps.title: Get Your Score
steps.desc: Instantly see your Strategy Score (0-100) along with detailed metrics for win rate, profit factor and drawdowns.

features.title: Risk-Adjusted Performance
features.desc: We don't just look at total profit. We analyze your returns relative to risk taken, giving you a true picture of your edge.

features.title: Win Rate vs Expectancy
features.desc: Understand the relationship between how often you win and your average win/loss size. This is the key to long-term profitability.

features.title: Drawdown Protection
features.desc: Analyze your worst periods to ensure your strategy is survivable during inevitable market shifts.

features.title: Profit Factor Insights
features.desc: Calculate your gross profit over gross loss. A profit factor above 1.5 indicates a high-probability professional system.

features.title: R-Multiple Normalization
features.desc: We convert all trades into R-units so you can compare performance across different account sizes fairly.

features.title: Actionable Insights
features.desc: Identify which symbols, sessions or trade types are dragging down your performance and which are your core winners.

faqs.q: What is a Trading Strategy Analyzer?
faqs.a: A trading strategy analyzer is a software tool used by professional traders to evaluate the statistical performance of their trading history. It identifies if profits are due to luck or a real edge.

faqs.q: How many trades are needed to evaluate a strategy?
faqs.a: Statistically, you need at least 30 trades for a basic signal, but 100+ are recommended for high-confidence results.

faqs.q: What is a good profit factor?
faqs.a: A profit factor greater than 1.0 means you are profitable. Above 1.5 is excellent, and above 2.0 is world-class.
Expectancy is the average amount you expect to win (or lose) per trade. It's calculated as (Win Rate × Average Win) − (Loss Rate × Average Loss).

sampleSection.title: What is a Trading Strategy Analyzer?
sampleSection.p1: A **Trading Strategy Analyzer** is the most critical tool in a professional trader's arsenal. While most retail traders focus on finding the 'perfect entry', professionals focus on **expectancy** and **risk-adjusted returns**.
sampleSection.p2: By using NodoQuant, you can determine if your recent winning streak was the result of a genuine **Trading Edge** or simply a run of luck. Conversely, an analyzer can show you if a losing streak is just normal statistical drawdown or a signal that your strategy no longer works.
sampleSection.h2_step: How to Analyze your Strategy Step-by-Step
sampleSection.p_step: Evaluating a strategy requires more than just looking at your account balance. Follow these steps:
steps: **Collect Data**: Export at least 50-100 trades from your broker.
**Normalize to R**: Convert your P&L to R-multiples (Risk units). This removes the bias of changing position sizes.
**Calculate Expectancy**: Use the formula: `(Win Rate % * Avg Win) - (Loss Rate % * Avg Loss)`.
**Identify Variance**: Review your maximum drawdown to see if you can handle the strategy's volatility.
sampleSection.h2_why: Why Strategy Analysis Matters for Your P&L?
sampleSection.p_why: Without analysis, you're flying blind. Many traders abandon good strategies during a normal drawdown because they don't understand their strategy's **Max Drawdown** profile.
sampleSection.proTip: Professional trading isn't about being right; it's about being profitable. An analyzer shifts your focus from the 'outcome' of a single trade to the 'process' of a series of trades.
sampleSection.ready: Ready to see your real numbers?
sampleSection.p_compare: While a trading journal is great for tracking your emotions, a **Strategy Analyzer** like NodoQuant focuses on quantitative output. Journals tell you *what* happened; Analyzers tell you *why* it's mathematically profitable.
sampleSection.finalLink: Upload your real trade data and analyze your strategy →

metadata.description: Test your crypto trading strategy with quantitative analysis. Upload your Binance, Bybit or exchange history and get Win Rate, Profit Factor, Expectancy and a Strategy Score in 60 seconds.

hero.headline: Does Your Crypto Strategy Have a Real Statistical Edge?
hero.subheadline: Upload your Binance, Bybit or exchange trade history and get a full quantitative analysis in under 60 seconds. Free, private and instant.

steps.title: Export your crypto history
steps.desc: Download your closed trades CSV from Binance, Bybit, Kraken or any exchange. Most offer this under Account → Trade History.

steps.title: Upload to NodoQuant
steps.desc: Drop the file into the analyzer. Our parser handles multiple exchange formats and normalizes columns automatically.

steps.title: Get your Strategy Score
steps.desc: Receive a full report: Strategy Score, Equity Curve, Win Rate, Profit Factor, Expectancy, Max Drawdown and statistical confidence rating.

features.title: Multi-Exchange Support
features.desc: Compatible with Binance, Bybit, Kraken, OKX and any exchange that exports trade history in CSV.

features.title: Strategy Score (0–100)
features.desc: A single score catching edge strength, risk-adjusted performance and how reliable your crypto strategy is.

features.title: Equity Curve Visualization
features.desc: See your cumulative P&L. Identify if your crypto strategy has an upward trend or was just a lucky streak.

features.title: Win Rate & Expectancy
features.desc: Calculate your true win rate and expected value per trade — the foundation of any profitable crypto system.

features.title: Drawdown Analysis
features.desc: Understand the worst drawdown your strategy produced to evaluate if it fits your risk tolerance.

features.title: Statistical Confidence
features.desc: Know if your results are statistically significant or just noise from a small sample of trades.

faqs.a: Any exchange that exports trade history as CSV — including Binance, Bybit, OKX, Kraken and Bitget. We also accept generic CSV formats.

faqs.q: Can I analyze both spot and futures trades?
faqs.a: Yes. NodoQuant analyzes both spot and derivatives (futures/perps) history from major exchanges.

faqs.q: How many crypto trades do I need for a reliable analysis?
faqs.a: At least 30 trades for basic results, 100+ for high-confidence evaluation. We display a warning if your sample is too small.

faqs.q: Is my crypto portfolio data secure?
faqs.a: Your upload contains only historical records — no API keys, balances or personal information are required.

faqs.q: Can I test my Bitcoin or altcoin strategies?
faqs.a: Yes. Any crypto pair is supported — BTC, ETH, SOL, altcoins and derivatives. Results are currency-agnostic.

features.desc: Forex markets are volatile. We calculate your worst peak-to-trough drop so you can manage your leverage safely.

features.title: Native Metatrader Support
features.desc: Our parser is optimized for MT4/MT5 reports. Simply drag and drop your file directly into the analyzer.

features.title: Statistical Confidence
features.desc: Never trust a strategy with a small sample size. We tag datasets with fewer than 50 trades to warn against randomness.

features.title: Strategy Score
features.desc: A single number from 0 to 100 telling you if your system is professional-grade, average or high-risk.

faqs.q: How do I analyze an MT4/MT5 strategy?
faqs.a: Go to the 'Account History' tab in Metatrader, right-click and select 'Save as Detailed Report'. Then upload that HTML or CSV file to NodoQuant.

faqs.q: Is analysis by pips or R-multiples better?
faqs.a: R-multiples are superior. Professional traders use R because pips don't account for stop-loss distance or position sizing.

faqs.q: Can I analyze data from Myfxbook or MetaStats?
faqs.a: Yes, if you can export the data to CSV. NodoQuant is designed to go deeper than basic journals, providing deep quantitative insights.

faqs.q: What is a good profit factor for Forex?
faqs.a: In the FX market, a profit factor between 1.2 and 1.8 is considered healthy. Anything above 2.0 is exceptionally strong.

faqs.q: Do you support EURUSD specifically for analysis?
faqs.a: Yes. You can use our symbol filtering to analyze only EURUSD trades and see if your edge differs between pairs.

content.h2_what: What is a Forex Strategy Analyzer?
content.p1: A **Forex Strategy Analyzer** is a quantitative tool designed specifically for the unique characteristics of the currency market. Unlike standard calculators, an FX analyzer must handle 24/5 liquidity and measure performance across different pairs.
content.p2: For Metatrader users (MT4/MT5), NodoQuant provides an automated way to transform a 'Detailed Report' into a professional quantitative audit.
content.p_why: The #1 reason Forex traders lose money isn't 'bad entries'. It's **poor risk management combined with a lack of statistical awareness**.
content.p_calc: By using our **Forex Expectancy Calculator**, you learn the limits of your strategy. If you know your system has a Max Drawdown of 15%, you won't panic at an 8% weekly loss.

card_mt.title: MT4/MT5 Compatibility
card_mt.desc: Export your history from Metatrader and we handle the rest. No manual input.

card_pair.title: Pair-wise Filtering
card_pair.desc: Analyze EURUSD strategies separately from your JPY trades. Find your niche.

content.p_master: EURUSD is the most liquid pair in the world. A **Forex Analyzer** allows you to see if your edge is consistent across sessions (London vs New York) and symbols.
content.ready: Analyze your Forex EA or Manual History
content.readyFoot: Supports Metatrader, TradingView and standard CSVs
content.metric_stability: Recovery Factor: Net Profit / Max Drawdown. Tells you how fast you recover.
content.p_leverage: Forex is a highly leveraged market. By analyzing your **Equity Curve**, you can visually see the impact of volatility and decide if your position sizing is responsible.
content.finalLink: Analyze your Forex strategy performance now →

metadata.title: Trading Edge Calculator — Know if Your Strategy is Profitable | NodoQuant
metadata.description: Calculate your trading expectancy and profit factor with our free calculator. Learn the formula for success and how win rate impacts your returns.
metadata.keywords: trading edge calculator, trading expectancy calculator, profit factor calculator, trading expectancy formula, calculate trading edge

hero.headline: Trading Edge Calculator — Know if Your Strategy is Profitable
hero.subheadline: Use our interactive tool to calculate your statistical edge. Discover your expectancy, profit factor and whether your risk-to-reward is sustainable.

steps.title: Enter Your Stats
steps.desc: Use the tool below to input your win rate, average win, and average loss to see your potential expectancy.

steps.title: Upload Real Data
steps.desc: While a calculator is great for estimation, NodoQuant lets you upload real history for a precise calculation based on performance.

steps.title: Identify your Edge
steps.desc: Our system calculates your Strategy Score, identifying if you have a genuine edge or if it's simply variance.

features.title: Expectancy Calculator
features.desc: Your expected profit or loss per trade. Positive expectancy is the bedrock of trading.

features.title: Profit Factor
features.desc: The ratio of gross profits to gross losses. Any value above 1.5 is considered top-tier.

features.title: Win Rate Analysis
features.desc: See how your win percentage interacts with your risk-reward. You don't need a high win rate to be profitable.

features.title: Drawdown Awareness
features.desc: Estimate your potential equity risk. Understanding drawdown is key to surviving losing streaks.

features.title: Edge Score (0–100)
features.desc: Get a unique score measuring the overall robustness and reliability of your trading edge.

features.title: R-Multiple Logic
features.desc: Learn to think in risk units (R) instead of dollars, the secret of professional traders.

faqs.a: An edge is a statistical advantage that over a large sample of trades makes your strategy profitable. It means the probability of success is higher than failure across time, combined with favorable risk-to-reward.

faqs.q: What is the trading expectancy formula?
faqs.a: Expectancy = (Win Rate × Average Win) - (Loss Rate × Average Loss). If the result is positive, your strategy has an edge.

faqs.q: What is a good profit factor?
faqs.a: Above 1.0 means you're profitable. Above 1.5 is great, and above 2.0 is professional level.

faqs.q: How many trades do I need for a reliable calculation?
faqs.a: At least 100 trades are recommended to have high statistical confidence. Small samples are often skewed by luck.

faqs.q: Can I use this calculator for Forex and Crypto?
faqs.a: Yes. Expectancy and edge calculations are market-agnostic. They work the same for Forex, stocks, crypto and futures.

content.p_interactive: Adjust the values to see how different metrics impact your strategy's expectancy.
content.p1: In the world of professional speculation, a **Trading Edge** is much more than a 'setup' or a signal. It's a **statistical advantage** that allows you to win over an extended series of trades.
content.p2: Having an edge means your **Expectancy** is positive. It implies that for every dollar you put up, you expect to receive that dollar back plus a surplus.
content.h3_how: How to Calculate Your Trading Expectancy
content.p_how: Expectancy is the holy grail of trading math. It combines your **Win Rate** and your **Risk-to-Reward Ratio**.
content.p_example: Example: If you win 40% of the time, and your average win is $300 while your average loss is $100:
content.p_result: This means you earn an average of **$60 per trade** across a large sample size.
content.p_calc_limit: A calculator is perfect for **planning** and **projecting**. However, real trading includes slippage, commissions and emotional errors.
content.p_reality: This is why professional traders use NodoQuant to analyze their **real history**. By uploading your trades, you move from 'theory' to 'reality'.

card_csv.title: Have 30+ trades in a CSV?
card_csv.desc: Stop guessing your stats. Get your real Strategy Score now.

content.h2_winrate: The Relationship Between Win Rate and R:R
content.p_winrate: A common mistake is obsessing over a high win rate. In reality, a strategy with a 25% win rate can be more profitable if the risk-to-reward ratio is high enough.
content.h3_metrics: Key Metrics for your Edge Report
content.finalLink: Calculate your edge with real trade data →

legal.contactUs: If you have questions about these terms, contact us at support@nodoquant.com
legal.compliance: NodoQuant complies with standard data protection regulations.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.sec1_content: By accessing and using NodoQuant, you agree to comply with and be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree with any part of these terms, you must not use our service.
terms.sec2_content: NodoQuant provides a software platform for quantitative analysis of historical trading data. The service includes visual tools, statistical metrics, and simulations based on user-provided data.
terms.sec3_content: NodoQuant IS NOT a financial advisor and does not provide investment advice. All tools and analysis are for educational and quantitative research purposes only. Trading involves substantial risk of loss.
terms.sec4_content: You are solely responsible for the accuracy of the data you upload to the platform. Errors in initial data files will lead to incorrect analysis results.
terms.sec5_content: NodoQuant shall not be liable for any financial loss, damage, or harm resulting from the use of our platform or trading decisions made based on our analysis.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.sec1_content: We collect information you provide directly to us, such as your name and email when you sign up or request an analysis. We also process the trading history files you upload to perform the quantitative analysis.
privacy.sec2_content: Your trading data is processed solely to generate your reports and statistics. By default, this data is private. It only becomes public if you explicitly choose to publish your strategy on our Leaderboard.
privacy.sec3_content: We implement technical and organizational security measures to protect your information. We do not share your trading data with third parties or brokers.
privacy.sec4_content: We use cookies to keep your session active and improve your browsing experience. You can disable cookies in your browser settings, although this may affect some site functions.
privacy.sec5_content: You have the right to access, rectify, or delete your personal information at any time. You can do this from your account settings or by contacting us directly.
